title: themelium conjunctisorum
side_effects: Themelium conjunctisorum can have side effects if used improperly, such as excessive drying of the skin, severe eye irritation, and allergic reactions. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can lead to skin lesions, blistering, and scarring. Ingestion of the plant's leaves or flowers can cause nausea, vomiting, and gastrointestinal distress, potentially leading to dehydration and electrolyte imbalances. Improper use of the plant can also trigger anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention.
